description_long: This is a delicate daisy-like flower with a single, slender green stem supporting a solitary bloom. The flower head features numerous elongated white petals, each tipped with a subtle pink hue, radiating symmetrically around a prominent yellow central disc. The stem is adorned with a few lanceolate green leaves, some of which show natural waviness and minor imperfections. The overall structure is thin and lightweight, with the flower positioned at the terminal end of the stem. The petals are arranged in a radial pattern, overlapping slightly to create a full, rounded appearance. This type of flower is commonly found in urban parks, gardens, and roadside green spaces.
description: A slender-stemmed daisy-like flower with white and pink-tipped petals radiating around a yellow center, accompanied by a few green lanceolate leaves.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full face of the flower, with petals radiating outward and two prominent green leaves visible on the stem.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the flower's profile, the yellow disc center, and a single leaf extending outward from the stem.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the flower head and the thin stem, with leaves partially obscured.
description_view_3: Angled rear view showing the underside of the flower and the attachment of the leaves to the stem.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial configuration, forming a single whorl around the central disc, with slight overlapping and clear radial symmetry.
